title: Getting started
description: From signing up to your first published chart: the essential steps to use Graph Italia. This is not the full documentation, just what you need to find your bearings: for everything else the interface guides you along the way.

account.title: Sign up and log in
items: Press Log in in the top right corner and create an account with your email: you will receive a verification code to activate it.
Every account starts with a personal project; you can create more and share them with other people through organizations.

create.title: Create a new item
items: From your private area press Create new and choose what to create: a chart, a KPI group, a map, a dashboard or a data source.
Give it a title and, optionally, a description right away (you can always change them later): the editor will open.

data.title: Load your data
items: In the Load your data step you can import a CSV or JSON file from your computer, or connect a remote URL.
With a remote URL the chart stays linked to its source: when public, the server re-downloads the data if the saved copy is older than 24 hours.

After loading you can clean the table up: filter and reorder columns, rename headers, transpose rows and columns. If the data has repeated rows, the editor suggests an aggregation (count, sum or mean).

configure.title: Configure the chart
items: Choose the visualization type (bars, lines, pie or geographic map) and watch the preview update in real time.
The options let you set direction, color palette, legend, height and an optional logarithmic scale; Advanced settings group tooltip, grid and axes.

publish.title: Save, publish and share
items: Press Save: the item appears in the lists of your private area.
In the Info section you decide its visibility: a public item is visible to anyone with the link and can be embedded in other websites; a private item stays visible to you only.
The Share column of the list has buttons to copy the public link and the embed code (iframe).

dashboard.title: Compose a dashboard
items: A dashboard gathers several charts and text blocks in a single page: drag the slots on the grid, adjust their width and height, write texts in markdown.
A dashboard is also shared with a single public link.

api.title: For developers
items: From the user menu you can generate an API key (read-only or read/write) to use the project's REST APIs.
Data sources are reusable datasets that can also be updated via API: useful to feed data from a script or an external system.

footer.text: That is all you need to get started: for everything else, each section of the application has contextual help texts.
